title: Notochloe microdon
taxonomy: Notochloe microdon is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Notochloe and is native to New Zealand. This plant is also known as 'toetoe', which is the Māori name for the grass. Notochloe microdon is a type of tussock grass that grows in a variety of habitats, including mountains and coastal areas. The classification of Notochloe microdon is further detailed in the scientific community as a member of the Poales order within the monocots.
uses: Notochloe microdon, a species of grass, has been found to have potential medicinal uses due to its antimicrobial properties. It has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat skin infections and wounds.
